expectations.text: States minimalist warm-ups are fine IF they reliably prepare you for the session
expectations.evidence: States: 'For many lifters, a 5-10 minute progression from empty bar to working weight is sufficient.'

expectations.text: Frames it as a cost-benefit issue (especially for time-limited lifters)
expectations.evidence: Discusses time efficiency and when minimalism works best.

expectations.text: Warns against under-warming if needed mobility/positioning work is removed
expectations.evidence: Lists 'Hidden Costs' including individual variation and genuine mobility restrictions.

expectations.text: Recommends iterative troubleshooting: remove one element at a time, keep only what helps
expectations.evidence: Does not explicitly recommend iterative removal approach. Gives general guidelines instead.

expectations.text: Concludes the best warm-up is the minimum effective warm-up for your body and session
expectations.evidence: Discusses matching warm-up to individual needs and limitations.
